title: bidens andicola var. decomposita
intro: Bidens andicola var. decomposita, also known as South American beggar ticks, is a plant species in the aster family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues, as well as being used as a treatment for various gastrointestinal problems. Bidens andicola var. decomposita can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, or used topically in poultices or salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
